name: Camunda Platform
what_it_does: Comprehensive process automation platform combining BPMN workflow orchestration and DMN decision management with visual modeling tools, human task management, and analytics for end-to-end business process automation.
use_cases: Building enterprise AI agent workflows with human-in-the-loop escalation paths modeled visually in BPMN for stakeholder review
Implementing decision automation using DMN decision tables where business rules change frequently and non-technical teams own rule updates
Orchestrating hybrid workflows that mix automated agent steps with manual human tasks, approvals, and external system integrations
Deploying AI-assisted process automation in regulated industries where BPMN audit trails satisfy compliance and audit requirements
Connecting agent pipelines to enterprise systems via pre-built Connectors for Slack, Salesforce, ServiceNow, and hundreds of other platforms
not_for: Lightweight automation tasks where the full Camunda Platform stack (Zeebe, Operate, Tasklist, Optimize) is disproportionate infrastructure overhead
Developer-centric teams who prefer code-first workflow definitions and find XML-based BPMN an impedance mismatch with their toolchain
Real-time sub-100ms latency requirements where gRPC job worker polling model introduces unacceptable delay
best_when: You need a complete enterprise BPM platform with visual modeling, human task management, analytics, and compliance features alongside programmatic workflow execution.
avoid_when: Your use case is purely technical automation without human tasks or business process governance requirements — lighter-weight orchestrators have less operational overhead.

auth.notes: Camunda 8 SaaS and self-hosted use OAuth2 client credentials via Camunda Identity; Camunda 7 (legacy) supports basic auth; API keys available for UI-based configuration; scopes control access to individual components (Operate, Tasklist, etc.)

pricing.free_tier_limits: Self-hosted unlimited; SaaS Starter plan free with limited process instances
pricing.paid_tiers: Professional and Enterprise SaaS tiers; Enterprise includes SLA, SSO, and dedicated support
pricing.notes: Zeebe engine and core components are Apache 2.0; Optimize (analytics) is not open source; cloud pricing is consumption-based on process instances executed

performance.rate_limits: SaaS rate limits vary by tier; self-hosted scales with Zeebe cluster size

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST and gRPC APIs return well-structured errors with problem detail format; BPMN error events enable domain-specific error handling within workflows; Operate UI provides rich execution diagnostics
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Process instance and job keys are globally unique and stable; Connector framework has built-in idempotency for outbound requests; message correlation keys prevent duplicate event processing
known_agent_gotchas: Camunda 7 and Camunda 8 are architecturally different platforms — documentation, SDKs, and deployment models do not transfer; verify which version your infrastructure runs before implementing
Connectors run as separate job worker processes — agent workflows using Connectors have an additional network hop and must account for Connector service availability
DMN decision evaluation is synchronous and blocking in the workflow thread — complex decision tables with large input datasets can become a throughput bottleneck
Process instance migration between BPMN versions requires explicit migration plan definition; there is no automatic migration for in-flight instances when deploying updated processes
Tasklist and Operate are separate deployments with separate databases; in self-hosted configurations, these services must be individually scaled and monitored

security_score_components.security_notes: Enterprise-grade security with OAuth2, component-level scopes, and secret management via Connector Secrets; HIPAA and ISO 27001 compliance available on Enterprise tier; self-hosted deployments require separate Identity service for full auth stack
